repeated_openings: I've had the best results when I stop optimizing for more agents and start optimizing for reviewable work units.
reasons: Owned content and outreach exist, but repo/public adoption signals are still low.
GitHub stars remain low, which suggests trust-to-free-use conversion is still the limiting step.
Proof assets and first-task framing likely matter more right now than more generic awareness work.
next_moves: Improve repo/site conversion surfaces before producing too much additional top-of-funnel content.
Keep publishing/distributing only when the asset clearly strengthens free use, trust, or adoption.
Reduce repetitive outreach patterns and keep messaging tied to real workflow pain.
Favor proof assets: examples, review bundles, first-task guides, and comparison pages.

four_marketing_questions.what_is_it: free and open-source tool that orchestrates existing agents on your machine
four_marketing_questions.who_is_it_for: developers/teams with engineering work too big to babysit and too risky to trust blindly
four_marketing_questions.why_different: repo-native unattended orchestration that aims to leave substantial, reviewable output instead of just a transcript
four_marketing_questions.why_now: free to use now and useful for overnight project-scale work while you sleep
